metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5066
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2: biochemical fractions of mouse brain homogenate showed RICS (ARHGAP32) was enriched in the PSD.

Figure 3: immunoprecipitation of RICS revealed association with β-catenin, N-cadherin, NMDA receptors, and PSD-95.

Figure 4: immunofluorescence labeling and confocal imaging showed RICS co-localized with β-catenin, PSD-95, and NMDA-R in cultured rat hippocampal neurons.. Experimental description: Results, Figure 4 legend: "Figure 4. Colocalization of RICS with β-catenin, PSD-95, and NMDA-R in cultured rat hippocampal neurons"

antibody:
"Antibodies to RICS were prepared by immunizing rabbits with peptides containing amino acids 670-735, 933-1009, 1518-1577 and 1675-1738, respectively. Antibodies were purified by affinity chromatography using columns to which the antigens used for immunization had been linked."

The canonical sequence for human RICS in uniprot "A7KAX9-1" is not the same as the sequence described in this paper in Figure 1: "Human RICS is a 1738-amino acid protein that contains a RhoGAP domain and three proline-rich motifs". This is uniprot accession "A7KAX9-2". The respective Rat protein is not so well annotated in uniprot at the moment and lacks this isoform. For this annotation, we selected uniprot accession F1MAK3 that is the same gene but perhaps not the exact same sequence that was targeted by antibodies in this paper.
